feed:
The world has been inching toward fully autonomous cars for years . In China, one company just got even closer to making it a reality. On Thursday, AutoX, an Alibaba(BABA) -backed startup, announced it had rolled out fully driverless robotaxis on public roads in Shenzhen. The company said it had become the first player in China to do so, notching an important industry milestone. Previously, companies operating autonomous shuttles on public roads in the country were constrained by strict caveats, which required them to have a safety driver inside. 
AutoX's cars are now 
But AutoX said its fleet of robotaxis are completely unmanned. That could mean an early test of China's ambitions for autonomous driving: To use the technology as a means of expanding urban mobility and solving traffic woes. Alibaba is already betting on the future possibilities. In May, it announced plans to invest $15 billion in Grab Holdings (GRAB) , Southeast Asia's leading ride-hailing firm. The deal will help Alibaba push its own delivery robotics business into international markets and provide technology for Grab's driverless car operations .
In a statement, AutoX CEO Jianxiong Xiao said the new development would “significantly reduce” traffic accidents in the country. It was also an example of how Chinese tech firms can leverage AI to make cities smarter — and greener, by reducing CO2 emissions 
In other words, AutoXs driverless robotaxi will be free of human intervention from now on and will operate completely independently. The company said it would provide the service from Monday through Friday during rush hours in Shenzhen's Nanshan District, a heavily populated area where there are high traffic volumes and many pedestrians.
